title: todo.rs + file/mod.rs: global singletons with std::sync::Mutex — denies per-session isolation, tests rely on escape hatches
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension:** Structural Modularity / Testability

**Location:** /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/tools/todo.rs lines 17-18 (TODO_LIST LazyLock), 85-90 (lock-then-clone_from); /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/tools/file/mod.rs lines 26-28 (READ_TRACKER LazyLock); /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/tools/bash/mod.rs lines 266-267 (BACKGROUND_SHELLS LazyLock); /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/tools/chainlink.rs line 7 (CHAINLINK_INSTALL_SHOWN AtomicBool)

**Violation:**
Four separate global singletons hold per-session state:
1. TODO_LIST (Mutex<Vec<TodoItem>>) — only ONE todo list exists in the entire process.
2. READ_TRACKER (Mutex<Vec<PathBuf>>) — only ONE read-tracker.
3. BACKGROUND_SHELLS (Mutex<HashMap<String, BackgroundShell>>) — all shells share one namespace.
4. CHAINLINK_INSTALL_SHOWN (AtomicBool) — 'once per session' but singleton means once per PROCESS.

Consequences:
1. **Multi-session parallelism is impossible.** Two concurrent sessions share the same todo list, leaked background shells, and the 'install shown' flag. This breaks any future plan to run subagents in parallel processes within the same binary.
2. **Tests cannot isolate.** reset_read_tracker exists at mod.rs:62 with #[doc(hidden)] to allow tests to reset — the presence of this escape hatch is proof the design is wrong. Tests that mutate the tracker interfere with each other when run in parallel; in practice developers will add #[serial_test] bandaids.
3. **No lifecycle.** On session end, nothing clears these structures. BACKGROUND_SHELLS leaks shells permanently until they self-finish.
4. **Difficulty of cleanup on panic.** A tool call that panics while holding a mutex poisons the global permanently — get_output() at mod.rs:172 even uses unwrap_or_else(PoisonError::into_inner) to paper over this.

**Mandated Refactor:**
1. Move all four pieces of state onto SessionState (or ToolContext), constructed once per session:
   struct SessionState { todos: Vec<TodoItem>, read_tracker: ReadTracker, shells: ShellManager, ... }
2. Pass &mut SessionState (or a Mutex-wrapped Arc) through ToolContext on every execute_* call.
3. Replace all 4 LazyLock globals with Default::default() initialisation inside SessionState::new().
4. Delete reset_read_tracker — tests construct fresh SessionState.
5. Have SessionState::drop() gracefully terminate all background shells and persist todos if configured.
